questions.question: Newcastle Disease, that was recently seen in news, is caused by which agent?
questions.explanation: Newcastle Disease is a highly contagious viral disease affecting poultry and wild birds, recently reported spreading across Europe and the United Kingdom (UK). It is caused by Avian Paramyxovirus Serotype 1 (APMV-1) and has global distribution. It occurs in three forms: lentogenic (mild), mesogenic (moderate), and velogenic (highly virulent). Transmission occurs via respiratory droplets, exhaled air, and feces of infected birds. Symptoms include respiratory distress (gasping, coughing), nervous signs (paralysis, twisted neck), and diarrhoea.

questions.question: Rongali Bihu is mainly celebrated in which state?
questions.explanation: The festive spirit of Rongali Bihu has spread across different parts of Assam. It is one of the most important festivals of Assam, also known as Bohag Bihu, marking the Assamese New Year and the arrival of spring. The term “Rongali” is derived from the word “Rong,” meaning joy, happiness, and celebration. The festival reflects the rich cultural traditions and social life of Assamese society. During celebrations, women wear traditional mekhela chador made of pat, muga silk, or cotton.

questions.question: Yuva Sangam is launched under which initiative?
questions.explanation: Registrations have been opened for institution-led exposure tours under Yuva Sangam Phase VI across paired regions. Yuva Sangam is a youth exchange programme that connects young people from different parts of India to promote national integration. It targets youth aged 18–30 years, including students, National Service Scheme (NSS) volunteers, Nehru Yuva Kendra Sangathan (NYKS) members, and young professionals. The programme is anchored by the Ministry of Education and implemented through Higher Education Institutions. It was launched under the Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat (EBSB) initiative to strengthen unity in diversity.

questions.question: Which city is the venue of 7th National Sub-Junior (Under-17) Boxing Championship?
questions.explanation: The 7th National Sub-Junior (Under-17) Boxing Championship has started in Nagpur from 4 April. The event was inaugurated by Nitin Gadkari. The week-long tournament has attracted around 600 young boxers from across India, reflecting its growing importance in the national sports calendar. It also serves as a selection platform, where top performers will be chosen for the Indian Under-17 squad. Selected players will represent India at the Asian Boxing Championship to be held in Tashkent later this year.

questions.question: The India–UN Development Partnership Fund was established in which year?
questions.explanation: The India-UN Development Partnership Fund Board of Directors recently met at India’s Permanent Mission in New York to review its operations, ongoing projects, and future plans. The fund was established in 2017 by India in partnership with the United Nations to support development projects in other developing countries, especially in the Global South. It is implemented by UN agencies in collaboration with partner governments and managed by the United Nations Office for South-South Cooperation. The fund promotes South-South Cooperation through demand-driven, country-led development initiatives aimed at shared prosperity.

questions.question: Which country is the host of One Health Summit 2026?
questions.explanation: The One Health Summit was held in Lyon on 7 April 2026, coinciding with World Health Day under the G7 French Presidency. It highlighted the One Health approach linking human, animal, plant, and ecosystem health. The Summit showcased the Quadripartite partnership of Food and Agriculture Organization, United Nations Environment Programme, World Health Organization, and World Organisation for Animal Health. It emphasized science-based cooperation, multilateralism, and strengthening global health surveillance systems.

questions.question: The Kalai-II Hydro Electric Project is proposed on which river?
questions.explanation: The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs, chaired by Narendra Modi, has approved the Kalai-II Hydro Electric Project on the Lohit River in Anjaw district of Arunachal Pradesh. The total investment for the project is ₹14,105.83 crore, with an estimated completion period of 78 months. The project will have an installed capacity of 1200 MW (6 × 190 MW and 1 × 60 MW) and is expected to generate about 4852.95 million units (MU) of electricity annually. It will be the first hydroelectric project in the Lohit Basin, helping in strengthening regional power supply, managing peak demand, and balancing the national grid.

questions.question: Mount Semeru, that was recently seen in news, is located in which country?
questions.explanation: Mount Semeru, the tallest and one of the most active volcanoes in Indonesia, recently erupted multiple times, sending ash plumes up to 1,100 metres high. Also known as Mahameru (“The Great Mountain”), it is an active stratovolcano (composite volcano) with a steep profile and frequent explosive eruptions. It is the highest peak on the island of Java and holds cultural and religious significance locally. It is located in East Java as part of the Tengger Massif, which also includes Mount Bromo.

questions.question: Which village has been declared a ‘profanity-free village’ in Madhya Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Borsar Village in Madhya Pradesh has emerged as a profanity-free village promoting respectful communication among residents. Villagers have collectively pledged to avoid abusive language in daily interactions. Awareness campaigns have been conducted to encourage discipline and positive behaviour across the community. The initiative is followed by people of all age groups, ensuring widespread participation. It has led to improved social harmony and a noticeable reduction in conflicts within the village.

questions.question: The ‘Watan Ko Jano’ initiative is organized by which ministry?
questions.explanation: A 250-member youth delegation from Jammu and Kashmir met Vice President C. P. Radhakrishnan at Uprashtrapati Bhavan. The delegation was part of the ‘Watan Ko Jano’ initiative organised by the Ministry of Home Affairs to promote national integration and exposure. The programme aims to strengthen the spirit of “Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat” through cultural exchange and awareness among youth.
